meta.certificationName: AWS Certified Generative AI Developer – Professional
meta.title: AWS-GAP-3: Bedrock Design, Evaluation, Safety and Operations

questions.stem: A team is building a chat application on Amazon Bedrock and wants to maintain a multi-turn conversation with a foundation model using a chat-oriented request structure. Which API should they use first?
questions.explanation: The Converse API is designed for conversational inference patterns. The other APIs serve agents, knowledge bases, or monitoring.

questions.stem: A workload needs straightforward single-request inference against a model without agent orchestration. Which Amazon Bedrock API is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: InvokeModel is the direct inference API for a model request. The other operations manage resources or evaluations rather than basic inference.

questions.stem: When selecting a foundation model for a new application, what should the engineer compare first?

options.text: Model capabilities and supported features against the workload requirements
Only the alphabetical order of model names
Only whether the model vendor has a logo

questions.explanation: Model selection should be based on capabilities, modalities, context, latency, and other workload-relevant features.

questions.stem: A product team needs grounded answers over internal documents rather than relying only on the model's pretrained knowledge. Which design pattern is most appropriate?

options.text: Retrieval-augmented generation with a knowledge base

questions.explanation: Knowledge-base-backed retrieval grounds responses on enterprise content. Monitoring and storage policies do not add retrieval context.

questions.stem: A Bedrock application must route model calls through a consistent configuration that can span Regions and model backends. Which Bedrock feature is relevant?
questions.explanation: Inference profiles help standardize inference routing/configuration patterns. The other services are not Bedrock inference routing features.

questions.stem: A developer wants the application to call business APIs as part of a generated workflow rather than only returning text. Which Bedrock capability best fits?
questions.explanation: Agents orchestrate model reasoning and action execution against connected systems. Guardrails and evaluation do not perform API actions.

questions.stem: Within an Amazon Bedrock agent, what is the primary purpose of an action group?

options.text: It defines callable operations the agent can use to fulfill tasks
It encrypts all prompts at rest

questions.explanation: Action groups expose operations and schemas that an agent can invoke. They do not replace IAM or serve as monitoring storage.

questions.stem: A team wants to standardize reusable prompts, versions, and deployment across multiple applications. Which Bedrock feature should they adopt?
questions.explanation: Prompt management is intended for reusable managed prompts and versions. The networking features do not manage prompts.

questions.stem: A chatbot should answer from an enterprise corpus stored in S3 and vectorized for semantic retrieval. Which Bedrock component is most directly involved?
questions.explanation: Knowledge Bases provide managed retrieval over enterprise documents using embeddings/vector search patterns.

questions.stem: A developer is debugging why an agent failed to complete a task that required an external API call. Which Bedrock concept should be inspected first?

options.text: The agent's action group definitions and permissions
The CSS theme of the frontend
The S3 bucket's static website setting

questions.explanation: Agent failures on tool use often come from action schemas or authorization issues. The other options are unrelated.

questions.stem: A company needs to block certain unsafe topics and redact sensitive content from generated responses across applications. Which Bedrock capability is designed for this?
questions.explanation: Guardrails enforce safety policies such as denied topics and sensitive content controls. The other features are not generative-AI safety controls.

questions.stem: Why should an engineer apply least-privilege IAM to generative AI applications built on Bedrock?

options.text: To restrict access to models and related resources to only what the workload requires
To increase model context window size

questions.explanation: Least-privilege IAM is a core security practice for controlling access. It does not affect context length or override guardrails.

questions.stem: A team uses a knowledge base over internal HR documents. What governance control is still important even if retrieval is managed by Bedrock?

options.text: Source data access and document lifecycle controls

questions.explanation: Managed retrieval does not remove the need to govern the underlying source documents and their access patterns.

questions.stem: A response contains disallowed content even though the prompt asked the model to avoid it. What is the best platform-level mitigation?

options.text: Apply or strengthen a Bedrock guardrail policy

questions.explanation: Guardrails are the platform feature for enforcing safety policies independent of prompt wording alone.

questions.stem: Which statement about IAM and Bedrock is correct?

options.text: Bedrock integrates with IAM for access control to service actions and resources
IAM is used only for billing reports

questions.explanation: Bedrock uses IAM for authorization. The other statements are false.

questions.stem: A team wants to compare candidate models against the same prompts and quality criteria before launch. Which Bedrock capability best supports this?
questions.explanation: Model evaluation is used to assess and compare models against defined criteria. The other features do not evaluate generative model quality.

questions.stem: Why is prompt versioning important in a production generative AI application?

options.text: It supports repeatability, rollback, and controlled change management
It removes the need for testing

questions.explanation: Versioning makes prompt changes auditable and reversible. It does not guarantee perfect model behavior or remove testing.

questions.stem: An evaluation shows a model gives fluent but factually weak answers on company policy questions. What is the best next design change?

options.text: Ground answers with a knowledge base or improve retrieval context

questions.explanation: Weak factuality on enterprise questions often points to insufficient grounding. Retrieval or better context is the right next step.

questions.stem: Which practice most directly helps detect regression after a prompt or model change?

options.text: Re-run a representative evaluation set and compare results

questions.explanation: Regression detection requires rerunning representative evaluations. Cosmetic changes do not validate model behavior.

questions.stem: A team has strict business rules for acceptable outputs. Why is human review still valuable even with automated evaluation?

options.text: Some quality and risk judgments are contextual and not fully captured by simple automated metrics
Automation makes all outputs legally approved
Humans are needed only for IAM setup

questions.explanation: Human review remains important for nuanced business risk and contextual correctness.

questions.stem: A prototype chatbot sends every user request to the most expensive model, causing unnecessary spend. What is the best first optimization principle?

options.text: Match model capability and cost to the task instead of always using the largest option

questions.explanation: Selecting the right-capability model for each workload is a primary cost-performance optimization.

questions.stem: An application experiences unpredictable latency because traffic spikes exceed the current inference path. Which Bedrock feature should the engineer review?

options.text: Inference profiles and model routing choices

questions.explanation: Inference configuration and routing choices are directly relevant to inference latency behavior.

questions.stem: What is the operational value of sending Bedrock metrics and logs to CloudWatch?

options.text: It enables visibility into usage, errors, and performance trends

questions.explanation: CloudWatch provides observability for usage, errors, and latency. It does not rewrite prompts or replace security controls.

questions.stem: A team wants to identify which prompts are causing timeouts or retries. Which approach is best?

options.text: Correlate application telemetry with Bedrock monitoring in CloudWatch

questions.explanation: Correlated observability helps trace failures to specific workloads and prompts. The other actions do not diagnose latency.

questions.stem: Why is it useful to benchmark latency and output quality together when evaluating models?

options.text: The cheapest or fastest model may not satisfy the application's quality requirements
Latency and quality are unrelated in production

questions.explanation: Model selection is a tradeoff across quality, latency, and cost, so measuring both is important.

questions.stem: A team needs image understanding as part of a support workflow. What must be validated before model selection?

options.text: That the chosen Bedrock model supports the required modality and features
That the model name is shortest
That the S3 bucket has website hosting
That the VPC has exactly two subnets

questions.explanation: Modality and supported feature checks are fundamental during model selection.

questions.stem: A developer wants agent responses to use enterprise search results plus external actions. Which combination is strongest?

options.text: Knowledge bases plus agent action groups

questions.explanation: Knowledge bases provide retrieval grounding and action groups let the agent perform tasks, together supporting richer workflows.

questions.stem: A company must prevent PII exposure in generated outputs. Which Bedrock control should be part of the design?

options.text: Guardrails configured for sensitive information policies

questions.explanation: Guardrails include protections relevant to sensitive information handling in generated content.

questions.stem: A prompt update improved helpfulness but increased policy violations. What is the best release decision?

options.text: Do not promote until the safety tradeoff is addressed and re-evaluated
Promote because helpfulness is the only metric that matters

questions.explanation: Release decisions should weigh multiple criteria, including policy adherence, not just helpfulness.

questions.stem: A knowledge-base answer quality is poor because relevant passages are not retrieved. Which area should the engineer examine first?

options.text: Data source quality, chunking, and retrieval configuration

questions.explanation: Poor retrieval quality often traces to source preparation and retrieval setup, not frontend browser settings.

questions.stem: Why is retrieval-augmented generation often preferred for rapidly changing enterprise policies?

options.text: It can ground responses in current documents rather than relying only on pretrained model knowledge
It removes the need for permissions

questions.explanation: RAG helps use up-to-date source content, which is useful when policies change frequently.

questions.stem: A team wants reusable prompts with approval before production rollout. Which Bedrock feature aligns best?
questions.explanation: Managed prompts support reuse and governance better than ad hoc console-only text.

questions.stem: An engineer says prompt instructions alone are enough to enforce safety. What is the best response?

options.text: Prompt instructions help, but policy enforcement should also use Bedrock guardrails and governance controls
That is always correct for regulated workloads

questions.explanation: Prompting is helpful but not sufficient as the only safety mechanism in enterprise workloads.

questions.stem: A model performs well in a lab test but fails when users ask ambiguous follow-up questions. Which evaluation gap is most likely?

options.text: The test set underrepresented realistic multi-turn scenarios
The S3 bucket name was too long

questions.explanation: If production issues appear in follow-up turns, the evaluation likely lacked representative conversation scenarios.

questions.stem: What is the best reason to instrument application-level latency in addition to Bedrock service metrics?

options.text: End-user experience also depends on retrieval, orchestration, and downstream integrations
Bedrock metrics are only for billing
Latency matters only for batch jobs

questions.explanation: Total experience includes more than model inference, so application telemetry is essential.

questions.stem: A workload requires domain-adapted behavior beyond prompt instructions alone. Which Bedrock capability might be evaluated if supported for the chosen model?

options.text: Custom model or model customization options

questions.explanation: Customization options may help when prompt-only steering is insufficient, subject to model support and workload needs.

questions.stem: A developer is deciding between calling a model directly or through an agent. Which criterion most strongly favors an agent?

options.text: The workflow needs tool use and task orchestration beyond simple text generation
The response must be in plain text
The documents are stored in S3

questions.explanation: Agents are especially useful when the solution must orchestrate actions and tools rather than only return generated text.

questions.stem: Which design choice reduces the blast radius if a compromised component tries to call unauthorized Bedrock actions?

options.text: Use narrowly scoped IAM permissions for each component
Share one admin role across every service

questions.explanation: Least-privilege IAM limits what a compromised component can do. Broad shared admin roles increase risk.

questions.stem: A team compares two models and one produces slightly slower responses but materially fewer policy violations. What should happen next?

options.text: Evaluate the tradeoff against business requirements instead of choosing on latency alone

questions.explanation: Selection should consider quality, safety, latency, and cost together based on the workload's requirements.

questions.stem: A generative AI service seems healthy in Bedrock metrics, but users still report slow answers. What is the best interpretation?

options.text: The bottleneck may be in retrieval, orchestration, or downstream systems rather than model inference itself
Knowledge bases cannot be used in production

questions.explanation: Healthy model metrics do not rule out latency elsewhere in the end-to-end application path.
